response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 15+ years of diverse experience, they are fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a 6-month tenure at National Instruments and academic work, insufficient for a mid-senior C++ specialist position. More critically, their career has focused on web/SaaS and full-stack development, with no demonstrated background in industrial automation, IoT protocols, secure development lifecycle practices, or IEC 62443 standards—all essential for this manufacturing automation role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals with demonstrated TDD, CI/CD, and agile practices across multiple roles
Proven leadership and mentoring capability through organizing workshops, technical talks, and open-source contributions
Extensive full-stack and polyglot programming experience across 15+ years with demonstrated ability to rapidly master new technologies
concerns: Minimal C++ experience—only 6 months dedicated C++ development; career heavily weighted toward JavaScript/TypeScript and D language
No demonstrated knowledge of industrial cybersecurity, SDL practices, or IEC 62443 standards required for manufacturing automation
Career background entirely in web/SaaS and developer tools; no exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, IEC 61850) or manufacturing domain

key_factors.factor: Secure development lifecycle and IEC 62443 experience
